reapack.name: reaper_reamix
reapack.desc: reamix.me: music-track retargeting via beat-aligned splicing

metadata.description: {\rtf1\ansi\deff0{\fonttbl{\f0 Calibri;}} \f0\fs28 reamix.me - Music-track retargeting for REAPER\line \fs22\line Pick any song, set a target length, and reamix produces a version at that length that sounds like a natural edit of the song - not a time-stretched version.\line \line The concept is Adobe Audition Remix, re-implemented as a native REAPER extension. The technique is beat-aligned splicing, not time-stretching: the algorithm finds pairs of beats where splicing from one to the other is perceptually inaudible (matched phase, chroma, energy, structural role) and stitches those splices into a path that hits the target duration.\line \line \b Three modes:\b0\line \bullet Target Duration - set a length, algorithm picks the optimal splice path\line \bullet Region Remix - retarget only the REAPER time-selection\line \bullet Block Assembly - manually order sections, algorithm optimizes the junctions\line \line \b Pipeline:\b0\line \bullet Neural beat detection (beat-this, ISMIR 2024)\line \bullet Structure segmentation (CBM, bar-level)\line \bullet 59-dim per-beat features (MFCC + chroma + spectral contrast)\line \bullet Transition cost matrix (10 perceptual signals + hard gates)\line \bullet Viterbi DP path optimization\line \bullet Multi-band crossfade rendering\line \line \b Requirements:\b0\line \bullet REAPER 6.0+\line \bullet Internet connection for first-time model download (~79 MB)\line \line \b Note:\b0 On first analysis, reamix.me downloads the beat-detection neural network (~79 MB) to a per-platform application-data directory. This happens once.\line }

version.author: b451c
version.changelog:
reamix.me 1.0.6 — Linux modal-dialog rendering polish
- Fix: "Add custom kind", "Rename block", "Delete block", and similar dialogs in Block Assembly rendered with static-noise artefacts behind their rounded corners on Linux. Root cause: JUCE's juce::AlertWindow paints a rounded-rect inside its local bounds and leaves the corner pixels untouched; macOS/Windows native peer windows pre-clear the backing buffer, but X11/Cairo on Linux does not, so the first frame showed uninitialised memory. Replaced juce::AlertWindow with a thin OpaqueAlertWindow subclass that fills the full bounds with the dialog background colour before the base paint runs. Affected dialogs (8 sites): Add/Rename/Delete custom kind, Change color, Rename/Delete block, Delete blocks.

version.author: b451c
version.changelog:
reamix.me 1.0.5 — Linux brand-logo polish + Region "Insert as new item" mode + CI cache speed
- Fix: brand logo rendered with jagged edges on Linux (Cairo/Pango) despite the 1.0.3 bicubic resampling fix. Replaced the bundled PNG with a true-vector SVG (42 rounded-rect rays + central hole baked into geometry); JUCE rasterises per render call so every backend (Quartz, Direct2D, Cairo/Pango) draws sharp at any size. - New: Region mode "Edit source in place" checkbox in the duration panel (visible only on the Region tab). Default ON preserves the existing splice-into-source behaviour. When OFF, the remix is inserted as a new media item immediately after the source on the same track — source clip stays untouched. Subsequent Inserts in this mode REPLACE the previously-appended clips (Update flow), matching the splice path's UX. - Internal: REAMIX_VERSION compile definition moved out of global compile flags into a generated header (src/version.h, configured by CMake). Version bumps no longer invalidate ccache for the ~200 unrelated translation units. Expected CI release-flow speedup: ~20 min → ~12-15 min wall. - Linux: section-kind picker popover marked opaque so X11/Cairo paints a solid background under the pill grid (no more rainbow-pixel artifacts behind the menu).

version.author: b451c
version.changelog:
reamix.me 1.0.4 — Linux MP3 decode fix
- Fix: "Unable to decode audio file" on Linux for MP3 inputs. JUCE's registerBasicFormats registers different decoders per platform — macOS uses CoreAudioFormat and Windows uses WindowsMediaAudioFormat (both decode MP3 via system codecs), but Linux has neither. Defined JUCE_USE_MP3AUDIOFORMAT=1 so JUCE's built-in MP3 decoder is registered everywhere; macOS and Windows still prefer their system codec, which remains first in JUCE's registration order — no regression. - WAV, FLAC, OGG Vorbis, AIFF continue to work unchanged on all platforms.

version.author: b451c
version.changelog:
reamix.me 1.0.3 — Preview UX polish + Windows visual fixes
- Fix: Preview volume defaulting to 0% on a fresh REAPER profile (Windows VM regression). REAPER's GetExtState returns "" for unset keys on some platforms; the preview_volume read now rejects empty strings as "not set" instead of parsing them as 0.0. - New: Welcome modal mentions SWS Extension as a (free, optional) dependency for Preview playback. - New: Startup-time SWS probe (~2.5s after plugin load) surfaces "Preview disabled — install SWS Extension" hint in the status bar when SWS is missing — so users learn this before clicking Preview and hearing silence. - Fix: Brand logo rendering on Windows. Source PNG is 128×124, target render is 18×18 in the header (≈7x downscale); switched to bicubic resampling (Graphics::highResamplingQuality) on both render sites for clean sun-burst rays on Direct2D/GDI. - README: SWS Extension listed under System requirements as a recommended optional dependency.

version.author: b451c
version.changelog:
reamix.me 1.0.2 — ReaPack coexistence fix
- Fix: ReaPack conflict with REABeat (or any future package shipping ONNX Runtime) — the ONNX Runtime library now installs to UserPlugins/reamix/ subdirectory instead of UserPlugins/ root. No more "already owned by another package" errors when installing reamix.me alongside REABeat. - Plugin RPATH searches both UserPlugins/ root (manual install convention) and UserPlugins/reamix/ (ReaPack install target), so both install paths work without code changes from the user. - Windows pre-load LoadLibraryW tries reamix\onnxruntime.dll first, falls back to root for manual installs.

version.author: b451c
version.changelog:
reamix.me 1.0.1 — Reliability + UX polish
- First-launch welcome modal with platform-specific setup hints (macOS Privacy & Security walkthrough, Linux libcurl install command) - Visible progress UI when downloading the AI model (status bar shows "Downloading · X / Y MB" with spinner, then "Loading model" during validation) - REABeat coexistence — if REABeat is already installed, reamix.me reuses its cached beat-detection model instead of re-downloading 80 MB - Startup error AlertWindow with platform-specific fix instructions on missing dependencies (libcurl, network, macOS Privacy & Security block) - ExtState persistence for welcome modal (shown only once per user)

version.author: b451c
version.changelog:
reamix.me 1.0.0 - First OSS release
- Native REAPER extension (single .dylib / .dll / .so, no Python, no server, no installer) - Three remix modes: Target Duration, Region Remix, Block Assembly - Neural beat detection (beat-this ONNX, ISMIR 2024) - 10-component transition cost matrix tuned for perceptual inaudibility - Multi-band crossfade rendering (bass 200 ms, mid 100 ms, treble 40 ms, phase-aligned) - Live REAPER integration: time-selection polling, undo blocks, group tracking, save/reopen - Interactive waveform editor with splice markers, region overlay, playhead - Advanced cost-weight panel (8 raw weights + 4 Block Assembly tuning parameters) - Keyboard shortcuts: Space (preview), Esc (stop), Enter (insert) - Per-track persistence: cost weights, mode state, defaults survive REAPER restart - Model auto-downloads on first use (~79 MB)
